rule: For ***INV*** about an invariant of the form ***`\\forall x \\cdot ... \\implies <goal about x>`***. In proving, `x0` means `x` after event. If the ***goal G is about `x0`***, then find the predicate that is the most similar to the invariant of this proof and instantiate it. e.g. if the goal is `x0=y`, look for `\\forall x0 \\cdot ... \\implies x0=y`, and instantiate it. Note that goal G `x0=y` is very similar to the implied predicate `x=y` in the hypothesis.
